channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Marina Silva fica na Rede e se coloca à disposição para disputar o Senado
item.description: Marina Silva confirmed her continued membership in the Rede Sustentabilidade party and made herself available to run for the Senate from São Paulo in 2026, potentially in a ticket with Simone Tebet (PSB) supporting Lula and Fernando Haddad. Her decision was based on a commitment to restore the party's founding principles, despite being courted by other parties including the PT, PSB, and Psol. Her affiliation remains under internal debate within the PT, which assesses party weight in determining slate positions.

item.title: Túlio Gadêlha se filia ao PSD, mas reafirma apoio a Lula
item.description: Federal Deputy Túlio Gadêlha (PE) left the Sustainability Network and joined the PSD on April 2, 2026, announcing his pre-candidacy for the Senate. Despite the party switch, he reaffirmed his support for President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva and the federal government, stating that his party change does not alter his political alignment. The affiliation ceremony took place in Caruaru (PE) with the presence of Governor Raquel Lyra (PSD), and Gadêlha said he aims to join the governor’s majoritarian ticket, although the final composition remains subject to political negotiations.

item.title: Veja onde assistir Londrina x Sport neste sábado (04)
item.description: Londrina and Sport face off this Saturday at 8:30 PM (Brasília time) at the VGD stadium, in the third round of the 2026 Brasileirão Série B. The match is crucial for both teams in their push for promotion to Série A.

item.title: Celina reúne lideranças e consolida apoio de 12 partidos
item.description: Governor of the Federal District, Celina Leão (PP), convened leaders from 12 political parties to solidify support for her 2026 re-election campaign. The meeting included representatives from União, MDB, PL, Republicanos, Podemos, Cidadania, Mobiliza, Democratas, DF, PRTB, and Agir, with notable attendees such as Bia Kicis (PL-DF) and Manoel Arruda (União Brasil-DF). Leão emphasized alignment on candidate lists and outlined government projects for the coming months, strengthening her political base ahead of the election.

item.title: Eduardo Bolsonaro bate-boca com Nikolas Ferreira pelas redes: 'Risinho de deboche para mim?'
item.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro accused Nikolas Ferreira of disrespect, of working against his father Jair Bolsonaro, and of betraying the political group that launched him, following an ironic comment by the deputy on social media. He said Nikolas had become a 'caricature of himself' and that fame had harmed him, especially for supporting those who wish his father dead. The feud began after Nikolas posted a pro-Bolsonaro video that Eduardo disliked, despite having previously criticized the 'Space Liberdade' profile.

item.title: Lula errou duas vezes, ao colar no STF nas vacas gordas e ao tentar se descolar nas vacas magras
item.description: Lula made two mistakes: first, by politically aligning with the STF and Alexandre de Moraes during the democratic consolidation period after January 8, creating the false perception of a Judiciary-PT conspiracy. Second, by now trying to distance himself from the scandal involving STF ministers and figures like Vorcaro, he resorts to vague accusations against Bolsonaro without evidence, revealing political weakness and risking institutional erosion.

item.title: Kátia Abreu anuncia filiação ao PT
item.description: Former senator Kátia Abreu announced her affiliation with the Workers' Party (PT) in a social media post on Saturday, the 4th. She stated she will join the 'fight for democracy and the re-election of President Lula', thanking PT members and the president himself for the invitation.

item.title: Frias endossa Eduardo e diz que Nikolas dá visibilidade a quem odeia o bolsonarismo
item.description: Federal Deputy Mário Frias (PL-SP) defended Eduardo Bolsonaro in a public feud with Nikolas Ferreira, accusing the latter of manipulating social media algorithms to boost the visibility of bolsonarism critics, even indirectly. Frias claimed Nikolas fully understands the consequences of his actions and that his behavior is a deliberate strategy to benefit ideological opponents, not innocence. Eduardo had previously accused Nikolas of giving visibility to those who wish death upon Jair Bolsonaro.

item.title: Marina Silva anuncia que ficará na Rede e disputará vaga no Senado por SP
item.description: Marina Silva announced she will remain with the Rede Sustentabilidade party and run for a Senate seat in São Paulo in 2026, ending speculation about her joining other parties. Her decision reinforces support for Lula’s re-election and Fernando Haddad’s gubernatorial campaign in SP, ruling out her potential role as Haddad’s running mate. A former Environment Minister, Marina was courted by at least eight parties but chose Rede to stay aligned with her environmental and democratic agenda.

item.title: Jair Renan solta indireta após treta entre Eduardo Bolsonaro e Nikolas
item.description: Jair Renan Bolsonaro posted an indirect quote from Olavo de Carvalho on social media following an argument between his brother Eduardo Bolsonaro and deputy Nikolas Ferreira. The dispute began when Nikolas responded with 'kkk' to a video critical of Flávio Bolsonaro, which Eduardo viewed as disrespectful to their family. Jair Renan's post, made 17 minutes after Eduardo's lengthy message, included the phrase 'Smirk of mockery is a whore's argument,' widely interpreted as aimed at Nikolas.

item.title: Frias critica Nikolas por promover opositores do bolsonarismo nas redes
item.description: Federal deputy Mario Frias (PL-SP) criticized Nikolas Ferreira (PL-MG) for using his social media interactions to amplify content critical of bolsonarism, claiming he 'trains the algorithm' to give visibility to opponents, even indirectly. Frias compared the practice to paid advertising in efficiency and accused Ferreira of using the 'dialogue outside the bubble' discourse as an excuse to benefit political adversaries. The statements were made on the same day that Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) criticized internal right-wing disputes and urged unity against the PT, with Ferreira sharing Flávio's video and captioning it 'Our party is Brazil'.

item.title: Marina anuncia permanência na Rede e se coloca 'à disposição' para 'segunda vaga ao Senado' na chapa de Haddad
item.description: Marina Silva announced she will remain in the Sustainability Network (Rede), reaffirming her commitment to Lula’s re-election and Fernando Haddad’s victory in São Paulo. She made herself available to be the second name on the Senate ticket, provided three conditions are met: support for Lula’s re-election, building a broad front in São Paulo, and advancing the green agenda. Marina stressed that her stay aims to strengthen Brazil’s democratic bioma and restore the founding principles of the Rede.

item.title: Entenda a nova treta entre Nikolas Ferreira e Eduardo Bolsonaro
item.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro criticized Nikolas Ferreira on X, calling him a 'caricatural version of himself' after Nikolas commented 'kkk' on a video about Pix. The exchange followed Eduardo's claim that members of the Space Liberdade channel would not vote for Flávio Bolsonaro, and Nikolas sharing a video asserting that Bolsonaro 'concretized' Pix in Brazil and labeling Lula a 'larápio'.

item.title: Erika Hilton diz que candidatos de direita querem privatizar o Pix. Entenda
item.description: Federal deputy Erika Hilton (PT-SP) accused right-wing candidates, including Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), of planning to privatize Pix to transfer control of the instant payment system to private banks, aiming to reintroduce fees. She argued that while they do not openly call for ending Pix, privatization would allow gradual taxation to recover banks’ losses from declining card and fee-based transfer usage.

item.title: Ligada a ruralistas, ex-senadora Katia Abreu se filia ao PT e é ventilada para disputar o governo do Tocantins
item.description: Former senator Kátia Abreu, tied to agribusiness and previously aligned with the right, announced her affiliation with the Workers' Party (PT) ahead of the 2026 elections, positioning herself as a potential candidate for governor of Tocantins. Although she opposed the PT during Lula's first term, she later moved toward the left, serving as Minister of Agriculture in Dilma Rousseff's government and being considered for a post at Banco do Brasil in 2023. The PT in Tocantins welcomed her as a strategic addition to broaden dialogue with various sectors, though the state party president avoided confirming her candidacy for governor.

item.title: “Não há limites para seu desrespeito”, diz Eduardo Bolsonaro a Nikolas
item.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro publicly criticized Deputy Nikolas Ferreira, accusing him of disrespecting his family and demanding that his group not support or promote Flávio Bolsonaro’s 2026 presidential candidacy. The social media post highlights internal tensions within the bolsonarist movement over electoral strategy and familial loyalty.

item.title: STF condena empresário de SC a 14 anos por atos de 8 de Janeiro
item.description: The STF sentenced Brazilian businessman Alcides Hahn to 14 years in prison for his role in the January 8 attacks, after he transferred R$500 to fund a bus that transported protesters from Blumenau to Brasília. Hahn was convicted on five charges, including violent abolition of the democratic state and criminal association, alongside two other financiers who did not travel. His defense claimed lack of proof of criminal intent, but the PGR upheld the case based on financial evidence and his alleged leadership in Bolsonaro-supporting mobilizations.

item.title: Briga de Nikolas e Eduardo Bolsonaro ganha novo capítulo: ‘Holofotes e fama te fizeram mal’
item.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro publicly accused his ally Nikolas Ferreira of disrespect and betrayal after the Minas Gerais deputy shared a social media post with three 'k's (kkk) in response to a publication about a right-wing profile that does not support Flávio Bolsonaro's presidential candidacy. Eduardo interpreted the 'kkk' as a mocking gesture directed at him and the Bolsonaro family, claiming Nikolas is actively working against those who supported him, including those who wish harm on Jair Bolsonaro. Nikolas has not yet responded, while other right-wing figures criticized Eduardo for overreacting.

item.title: Pré-candidato, Haddad evita citar Tarcísio e credita obras em SP a Lula
item.description: Fernando Haddad (PT), pre-candidate for governor of São Paulo, avoided directly naming Tarcísio de Freitas in his campaign videos but emphasized that state investments stem from federal actions under President Lula, including direct transfers, BNDES financing, loan guarantees, and state debt renegotiation — measures he claims he led as former Finance Minister. Haddad framed the Lula government’s partnership as being with the people of São Paulo, indirectly criticizing Tarcísio for claiming credit for works without acknowledging their federal origins. He stated that São Paulo needs a genuine alternative committed to real public policies.

item.title: Nikolas cobra Alcolumbre para analisar veto ao PL da Dosimetria
item.description: Federal deputy Nikolas Ferreira (PL-MG) urged Senate President Davi Alcolumbre (União Brasil-AP) to schedule analysis of the presidential veto on the Dosimetria Bill, calling the delay an 'omission' and a 'humanitarian urgency'. He accused Alcolumbre of stalling Congress to avoid installing the Banco Master CPMI, linking the issue to an alleged deal between Valdemar Costa Neto and the government to overturn vetoes in exchange for blocking the investigation.

item.title: Ruralista, Kátia Abreu decide se filiar ao PT
item.description: Former senator and ex-Minister of Agriculture Kátia Abreu announced her affiliation with the PT in Tocantins, following a trajectory marked by early opposition to Lula’s governments and later alliance with the party in 2022. She emphasized her commitment to democracy and Lula’s re-election in a video alongside state party leaders. Her career includes stints in PFL, DEM, PSD, MDB, PDT, and PP, and she is the mother of Senator Irajá (PSD-TO).

item.title: ‘Lei da misoginia’ causa dúvidas nas redes; veja explicações de especialistas
item.description: The Brazilian Senate approved a bill criminalizing misogyny as a form of prejudice, equating it with racism under national law. The proposal targets acts driven by hatred or contempt toward women as a group — such as inciting violence or workplace discrimination — but excludes respectful disagreements. It must still be passed by the Chamber of Deputies and signed by the President to take effect.

item.title: Nunes Marques viajou em jatinho de advogado de Cláudio Castro
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Kassio Nunes Marques confirmed he traveled on a private plane owned by lawyer Gustavo Severo, who works on cases at the TSE. He stated he maintains a friendship with the lawyer and declared himself disqualified in related proceedings. The information was reported by Folha and confirmed by the minister.

item.title: Flávio critica “picuinhas” na direita e diz que pode libertar país do PT
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro (PL), a pre-candidate for president, stated he is in the position to liberate Brazil from the 'dirty claws of the PT', criticating internal right-wing 'nitpicking' over minor interests and claiming former President Jair Bolsonaro was unjustly treated. He pledged to rescue the country, put it on the path to prosperity, and honor Bolsonaro’s legacy, urging supporters to join him.

item.title: Celina Leão sentiu ‘força contrária’ do governo federal para solucionar caixa do BRB
item.description: Governor Celina Leão (PP) of the Federal District stated that the federal government is exerting a 'contrary force' to block a solution to the BRB's R$ 12 billion cash shortfall stemming from illegal operations with Banco Master. She appealed to Finance Minister Dario Durigan for federal support, including Caixa Econômica Federal's backing for a R$ 4 billion FGC loan and R$ 4 billion in funding from state-owned banks, but said the support has not materialized. Leão, who plans to run for re-election in 2026, backs Flávio Bolsonaro’s candidacy and denies any involvement in the BRB-Master scheme.

item.title: Nunes Marques viajou em avião executivo de advogado que defendeu Cláudio Castro e atua no TSE, diz jornal
item.description: Minister Kassio Nunes Marques traveled at least five times in September 2025 on an executive jet owned by lawyer Gustavo Severo, who practices before the TSE and previously defended Claudio Castro. The minister stated he always declares himself disqualified in cases where Severo acts, and that these recusals were recorded prior to the flights. Severo withdrew from Castro’s case when it moved to the TSE, where Nunes Marques and André Mendonça voted to acquit the former governor.

item.title: A preocupação de José Dirceu com o tema da corrupção nas eleições
item.description: Former minister José Dirceu expressed concern over the rising prominence of corruption in the 2026 elections, noting it is now a top concern for 20% of voters, up from 13% in May 2025, according to Genial/Quaest. He warns that perceptions of rising corruption, tied to scandals like Banco Master and INSS fraud, are fueling anti-system voting and could benefit opponents such as Flávio Bolsonaro, who plans to weaponize the issue against Lula.

item.title: Celso Sabino anuncia, em Belém, pré-candidatura ao Senado pelo PDT
item.description: Federal deputy and former Tourism Minister Celso Sabino announced his affiliation with the PDT in Belém and declared his pre-candidacy for the Senate from Pará in the 2026 elections. After being expelled from União Brasil in 2025 for refusing to leave the Lula government despite party orders, he stated he was ousted for defending the 'best project' for the party and government. His background includes a renewed mandate in 2022 and appointment to the Tourism Ministry in 2023.

item.title: Defesa de Bolsonaro envia relatórios médicos ao STF que indicam cirurgia no ombro
item.description: The defense of former President Jair Bolsonaro submitted two medical reports to the Supreme Federal Court (STF) indicating the need for shoulder surgery. The documents were presented as part of a request to suspend or adapt ongoing judicial measures, citing a health condition that allegedly impairs his full exercise of rights. The move comes amid ongoing proceedings against Bolsonaro in the STF, including investigations related to anti-democratic acts.

item.title: Defesa de Bolsonaro envia parecer médico ao STF com indicação de cirurgia no ombro
item.description: Bolsonaro's defense submitted two medical reports to the STF indicating the need for right shoulder surgery, citing intense pain and functional limitation. The documents, signed by a physiotherapist and a doctor, were sent in compliance with Justice Alexandre de Moraes' requirement for regular health updates on the former president, who is under house arrest following his conviction for attempting a coup.

item.title: A contradição de Michelle e Carlos sobre o estado de saúde de Jair Bolsonaro
item.description: Carlos Bolsonaro claimed his father, Jair Bolsonaro, was suffering from endless hiccup crises and rapidly deteriorating health under house arrest, while Michelle Bolsonaro stated three days later that he had been hiccup-free for six days and was undergoing physiotherapy, creating a clear contradiction. This inconsistency, exploited by Carlos to advance a narrative of judicial persecution against Bolsonarism, undermines the family's credibility before the STF and the public.

item.title: “Senador pensa que é Deus”: fala de Lula amplia tensão com o Congresso e pode custar caro
item.description: President Lula da Silva sparked political tension by stating that senators 'think they are God,' a remark analysts say undermines government coordination, particularly as it seeks approval for Jorge Messias' nomination to the Supreme Federal Court. The comment was seen as an attack on legislative independence and risks damaging Lula’s standing with voters who strongly support democratic institutions.

item.title: Comitê da Câmara dos EUA fala em censura do Brasil contra Trump
item.description: The U.S. House Judiciary Committee claims that Brazil, through actions by Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes, has issued censorship requests to digital platforms to remove content praising Donald Trump and criticizing Joe Biden and USAID, alleging that this undermines U.S. sovereignty and could interfere in Brazil’s October 2026 elections. The report, the third in a series dating back to 2024, relies on data provided by Elon Musk’s X (formerly Twitter) and involves Trump allies like Jim Jordan and members of the Bolsonaro family.

item.title: Lula e Flávio Bolsonaro torcem por interferência de Trump nas eleições
item.description: Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro, tied in polls for the 2026 elections, both see potential benefit in possible interference by Donald Trump in the Brazilian campaign: the senator openly appeals for U.S. support to ensure 'free and fair elections,' while Lula could use such interference to revive his national sovereignty discourse, as he did after the U.S. tariff increase in 2023.

item.title: PL amplia hegemonia, PT sofre baixa e União Brasil lidera perdas na Câmara
item.description: The PL expanded its dominance in the Chamber of Deputees after the party-switching window, reaching 101 deputies and solidifying as the largest bloc, driven by 17 new entrants. União Brasil suffered the steepest losses, with 18 departures and only two additions, falling to 44 deputies, while the PT remained stable at 66 despite Luizianne Lins leaving for Rede.

item.title: Clica e Confirma destaca importância da atuação de mesárias e mesários nas Eleições 2026
item.description: The Clica e Confirma program highlights the importance of poll workers (mesárias and mesários) in the 2026 elections, explaining how citizens can volunteer through the Electoral Justice Portal and TRE websites. It also covers key electoral dates, including the end of salary adjustments for public servants starting April 7, the deadline for voter registration regularization on May 6, and the closing of registrations for National Electoral Observation Missions on July 5. Questions can be sent to ain@tse.jus.br.

item.title: A maior fragilidade de Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro, senator from PL-RJ and eldest son of Jair Bolsonaro, became the de facto candidate of Bolsonarism in the 2026 elections after his father's conviction and imprisonment for attempted coup. Although he absorbed his father's voting intentions, he remains largely unknown to the majority of the Brazilian population, which constitutes his greatest weakness as a pre-candidate. His campaign relies on the transfer of political capital from his father, but he lacks the recognition and autonomy to build a solid electoral base.

item.title: Michelle celebra Bolsonaro há 6 dias sem soluços
item.description: Michelle Bolsonaro celebrated on Instagram that her husband, Jair Bolsonaro, has been hiccup-free for six days and is able to undergo physiotherapy, following 14 days of hospitalization for bacterial bilateral pneumonia. The former president is serving a 90-day house arrest in Brasília, and his legal team submitted new health documents to the STF indicating a need for shoulder surgery due to chronic pain. Carlos Bolsonaro had stated three days prior that his father was still suffering from uninterrupted hiccup crises.

item.title: Michelle contradiz Carlos e diz que Bolsonaro está há 6 dias sem soluços
item.description: Michelle Bolsonaro stated that Jair Bolsonaro has gone 6 days without hiccups and is undergoing physiotherapy, contradicting her son Carlos’s recent claim of persistent, uninterrupted hiccup episodes. Bolsonaro has been under house arrest since March 27 following discharge for severe bilateral bacterial pneumonia, and his legal team submitted to the STF a physiotherapy report recommending shoulder surgery due to chronic pain.

item.title: Novo levantamento do Paraná Pesquisas mostra como anda popularidade do STF no país
item.description: According to a Paraná Pesquisas survey conducted between March 25 and 28, 42.3% of Brazilians rate the STF's work as 'bad or terrible', 27.9% as 'regular', and 24.4% as 'excellent or good'. The poll interviewed 2,080 voters across 26 states and the Federal District, with a 2.2 percentage point margin of error and 95% confidence level.

item.title: O que revelam as pesquisas sobre Flávio x Lula no estado que costuma definir eleições
item.description: Minas Gerais is considered a 'Brazil in miniature' due to its socioeconomic and regional diversity reflecting the nation as a whole, with polls showing a technical tie between Lula (43.7%) and Flávio Bolsonaro (40.4%) in the first round and 47.3% to 46.9% in the second. The state has historically accompanied the presidential election winner since redemocratization, making it decisive in defining the national outcome.

item.title: Lula ainda está devendo nas redes sociais
item.description: Former President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva showed weaker social media performance compared to Bolsonarist allies between March 20 and 26, according to a FGV Comunicação survey. While Lula’s official Instagram profile recorded 451,000 interactions, the profiles of Nikolas Ferreira and Flávio Bolsonaro each reached 1.2 million. The analysis highlights the digital weakness of the PT leader against the strong online presence of right-wing figures.

item.title: Os votos que faltam a Messias
item.description: Jorge Messias, the Attorney General of the Union, is seeking senatorial support for his nomination to the STF, needing 14 votes in the Constitution and Justice Committee but currently lacking sufficient backing. Tensions between Messias and STF President Edson Fachin are hindering the approval of the Court’s administrative plan. Meanwhile, the Master case investigation raises concerns about potential nullities and is fueling impeachment efforts against justices such as Alexandre de Moraes and Dias Toffoli.

item.title: Rui Costa deixa comando da Casa Civil com acúmulo de atritos na Esplanada e distante do posto de sucessor de Lula
item.description: Rui Costa stepped down as Chief of Staff after three years and three months without achieving national political prominence or positioning himself as a likely successor to Lula, due to conflicts with other ministers and a management style focused on operational checklists rather than strategic vision, reminiscent of Dilma Rousseff’s tenure. His oversight of the PAC was undermined by budget cuts that shifted focus from major infrastructure projects to localized, electorally beneficial initiatives like health clinics and ambulances, and he faced notable tensions with Finance Minister Fernando Haddad.

item.title: Kassio viaja em avião de advogado de Cláudio Castro e que atua no TSE
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Kassio Nunes Marques has traveled multiple times on the private plane of electoral lawyer Gustavo Severo, who works at the TSE. Kassio himself currently serves as president of the Superior Electoral Court.

item.title: Executivos priorizam pessoas, IA e eficiência em ano eleitoral incerto
item.description: A study by ANK Reputation involving 125 Brazilian executives found that only 14% of companies are truly prepared to avoid reputational damage in crises, while 70% say they are prepared but need to improve, and 16% are either just starting or lagging in this area. Despite 54% of leaders ranking reputation as a top priority for 2026 (up from 35% in 2025), most still view external risks — such as elections, geopolitics, AI, and societal polarization — as more dangerous, underestimating internal risks like leadership and employee misconduct (only 11% of mentions). Founder Anik Suzuki warns that the lack of a preventive culture leaves companies vulnerable, as crisis costs far exceed prevention investments.

item.title: Deputado troca de partido duas vezes em menos de um mês, durante a janela partidária
item.description: Federal deputy Nelsinho Padovani from Paraná, elected in 2022 under União Brasil, changed parties twice in less than a month during the 2025 partisan window. He joined the PL on March 5, the first day of the window, in an event alongside Flávio Bolsonaro and Valdemar Costa Neto. The article is subscriber-only and requires registration for full access.

item.title: 17 ministros deixam governo Lula para disputar eleições; veja lista
item.description: President Lula's administration has dismissed 17 ministers who are set to run in the 2026 elections, including Fernando Haddad (for São Paulo governor), Simone Tebet (for São Paulo Senate seat), and Gleisi Hoffmann, following the TSE-mandated six-month incompatibility deadline. Recent polls show 52% disapproval of Lula’s government and a technical tie between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro in a potential runoff. The departures were formalized in the Official Gazette, with interim replacements appointed to ensure administrative continuity.

item.title: Por que Fernando Haddad
item.description: Fernando Haddad is presented as a progressive candidate for governor of São Paulo in 2026, backed by Lula and popular forces, due to his commitment to a sovereign, democratic, and inclusive national project. The article argues his governance would be a necessary alternative to Tarcísio de Freitas’s administration, criticized for police violence, education dismantling, Sabesp privatization, and abusive highway tolls.

item.title: Leonardo Barreto na Crusoé: Todos os cenários de Kassab
item.description: Gilberto Kassab chose Ronaldo Caiado as a backup option after Ratinho Jr. withdrew, who had been seen as the PSD's ideal candidate due to his centrist positioning between left and right. Caiado's selection reflects a strategic 'Plan B' to keep the party competitive in the 2026 elections, despite his stronger alignment with the conservative camp. The move indicates a tactical adaptation by the PSD in the absence of a more neutral figure.
